title: ICD clinic documentation

red_flags.text: Supports recording received alerts, priority assigned by a qualified clinic, review, communication, and emergency-system limitations without generating alert programming, urgency, diagnosis, or treatment.
red_flags.rationale: The alert section supports recording received alerts, qualified review, patient communication, and clinic-defined action while emphasizing that remote monitoring is not an emergency system; it does not assign urgency or diagnose an event automatically.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording received alerts, priority assigned by a qualified clinic, review, communication, and emergency-system limitations without generating alert programming, urgency, diagnosis, or treatment.

source.title: 2023 HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS expert consensus statement on practical management of the remote device clinic

exact_section.heading: Section 8 - alert definition, review, and communication

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The workflow section supports documenting that device data were received, triaged, reviewed, signed off, communicated, and linked to a clinician-entered plan without interpreting the data automatically.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.

source.title: 2023 HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS expert consensus statement on practical management of the remote device clinic

exact_section.heading: Sections 4-5 - staffing, data triage, review, final sign-off, documentation, and communication

follow_up.text: Supports documenting device and monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, patient education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device status.
follow_up.rationale: The multisociety section supports documenting ICD and remote-monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device function.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting device and monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, patient education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device status.

source_limitations: Device type and manufacturer, implant indication and date, leads, battery, sensing, pacing, therapies, arrhythmia episodes, alerts, connectivity, transmission date, technical quality, symptoms, formal interpretation, programming, diagnosis, treatment, communication, emergency plan, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
